title: Managed-device configuration-profile detail
description: One record per managed device across an MDM (Jamf Pro managed Mac, Microsoft Intune managed device): the bounded list of configuration / compliance profiles deployed to the device, each carrying the profile name, identifier, type/category, assigned device-group scope, UUID, last-modified/deployed timestamp, and a bounded list of compliance-relevant settings reported as non-secret summary values (e.g. 'passcode_required: true', 'disk_encryption_enforced: true', 'firewall_enabled: on'). This evidence answers the configuration-management control question ('show the screen-lock policy that is enforced', 'show the disk-encryption configuration profile') at a finer grain than the endpoint.device_posture.v1 posture verdict; it is therefore a SEPARATE evidence kind, sibling to endpoint.device_posture.v1 (slice 490) and endpoint.software_inventory.v1 (slice 555), not an extension of either. Pulled read-only from the MDM API (Jamf configuration-profiles + computer config-profile assignments; Intune deviceConfigurations / deviceCompliancePolicies). THE LOAD-BEARING SECRET-REDACTION BOUNDARY (P0-556): configuration profiles routinely embed SECRETS — Wi-Fi PSKs, VPN shared secrets, certificate private keys, API tokens, SCEP challenges, and arbitrary password / <data> / PayloadContent payload values. NONE of these ever enters an evidence record. The per-profile settings field is restricted to a compliance-relevant ALLOW-LIST whose values are non-secret summaries (booleans / enums / small integers); the vendor clients request only profile metadata + a settings summary at the API boundary, never the raw payload-content blob; and the record builder re-applies the allow-list. The connector reports a descriptive configuration baseline; the platform evaluator owns the final pass/fail per (control, scope). Shared across the Jamf + Intune connectors because the two MDMs' configuration-profile field set is identical at the detail-summary altitude; the source_mdm field preserves provenance.

source_mdm.description: MDM the record was collected from.

device_id.description: MDM-native stable identifier of the managed device (opaque, non-secret). Joins to the device's endpoint.device_posture.v1 record.

profile_count.description: Number of configuration / compliance profiles emitted for this device (length of the profiles array, after the per-device bound is applied).

profiles.description: Bounded list of configuration / compliance profiles deployed to the device.

identifier.description: Optional profile identifier (Jamf payload identifier / Intune policy id). Opaque, non-secret.

profile_type.description: Optional profile type / category (e.g. 'configuration', 'compliance', 'Restrictions', 'FileVault'). Descriptive metadata.

scope.description: Optional assigned device-group / assignment NAMES the profile is deployed to. Group names only — NEVER owner personal contact detail.

uuid.description: Optional profile UUID the source reports. Opaque, non-secret.

last_modified.description: Optional last-modified / deployed timestamp the source reports (RFC3339 or the source's own date form). Descriptive.

settings.description: Bounded list of compliance-relevant settings the profile enforces, reported as non-secret summary key/value pairs. Restricted to a compliance-relevant ALLOW-LIST: keys that could carry a credential (Wi-Fi password, VPN shared secret, certificate private key, SCEP challenge, API token, raw payload-content blob) are NEVER present (P0-556).

key.description: Compliance-relevant setting key from the connector allow-list (e.g. 'passcode_required', 'disk_encryption_enforced', 'firewall_enabled', 'screen_lock_enforced').

value.description: Non-secret summary value for the setting (e.g. 'true', 'enforced', '8'). NEVER a credential value.
